classification_consistency_check.reason: Overall score 2.38 yields V5 band 'Needs Work' (equivalent to V6 'Remediation Required'), but V6 severity counts (1 High) map to 'Pilot-Ready'. The single High finding (SEC-Q1: audit logging) is a genuine gap but does not represent systemic modernization deficiency — 6 of the 7 Medium findings reflect expected characteristics of a library repo_type (no API auth, no identity integration, no distributed tracing) rather than actionable remediation items.

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No distributed tracing instrumented in the library
top_gaps.impact: Consumers cannot trace operations through gulp in their distributed builds

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No audit logging for library operations
top_gaps.impact: No structured logging of task execution or file transformation events

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: No API authentication (library exposes no HTTP API)
top_gaps.impact: Expected for a local build tool — no network surface exists

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.impact: Expected for a local build tool — no user identity handling

top_gaps.title: Monolith vs Microservices
top_gaps.gap: Single monolithic class coupling all functionality
top_gaps.impact: Limited decomposability; all functionality in one Gulp constructor

findings.title: No distributed tracing instrumentation
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry SDK, X-Ray instrumentation, or trace ID propagation. The library does not emit traces or propagate trace context through its streaming pipeline.
findings.gap: Consumers cannot trace operations through gulp's task execution or file streaming pipeline. In CI/CD environments where gulp runs as part of a larger distributed build system, this prevents end-to-end visibility.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding optional OpenTelemetry instrumentation for task execution spans. Implement as an opt-in plugin or optional dependency to avoid adding overhead for users who don't need tracing.

findings.title: No audit logging infrastructure
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent logging configured. As a library published to npm, there is no runtime infrastructure to audit. The library itself does not produce audit logs for its operations.
findings.gap: No audit logging for library operations. No structured logging of task execution, file operations, or build activities.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding structured logging output (via a logging interface that consumers can configure) for task execution events. For a build-tool library this is a low-priority enhancement.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: The library exposes no HTTP API endpoints. It is consumed programmatically via require/import. There is no network-facing surface to authenticate.
findings.gap: No API authentication — but this is expected for a build-tool library with no network surface. This score reflects absence rather than a security vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. A build-tool library consumed locally does not require API authentication.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No centralized identity provider integration. The library does not handle user identity or authentication. It runs as a local build tool.
findings.gap: No identity integration — expected for a local build tool library.
findings.recommendation: No action needed. A local build-tool library does not require centralized identity integration.

findings.title: Monolithic library design
findings.description: Single npm package that delegates to 4 tightly-coupled core dependencies via prototypal inheritance and property assignment in a single Gulp constructor.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit with direct coupling to all 4 dependencies. No interface abstraction between the core modules.
findings.recommendation: For a library of this size (~60 lines of core logic), the current single-package architecture is appropriate. No decomposition needed.

findings.title: No formal API versioning beyond semver
findings.description: The library uses semver versioning (v5.0.1) but has no formal API versioning strategy within the exported surface — no versioned interfaces, no deprecation annotations.
findings.gap: No formal API versioning strategy beyond npm semver. No deprecation annotations or versioned interfaces.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding JSDoc @deprecated annotations for methods planned for removal in future majors, and documenting API stability guarantees per export.

findings.title: No dynamic plugin discovery
findings.description: Dependencies are hardcoded require statements. Plugin ecosystem relies on naming convention (gulp-*) only, with no programmatic discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: No dynamic plugin discovery mechanism. Plugins loaded by consumers, not discovered dynamically.
findings.recommendation: For a build-tool library, the current require-based dependency resolution is appropriate. No architectural change needed.

findings.title: No automated vulnerability scanning
findings.description: CI/CD pipeline uses GitHub-managed runners (auto-patched) but has no explicit vulnerability scanning. Package.json overrides suggest manual security awareness but no systematic approach.
findings.gap: No automated vulnerability scanning in CI/CD. Dependency version overrides are manual rather than automated.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot or npm audit to the CI pipeline to automate dependency vulnerability detection.

findings.title: Legacy JavaScript code patterns despite modern Node.js requirement
findings.description: Node.js >= 22 required but source uses var declarations, util.inherits(), and callback-style APIs rather than ES6 classes, const/let, and async/await.
findings.gap: Language version is modern (Node.js 22+) but code style is legacy JavaScript. Framework/SDK lag signal.
findings.recommendation: Consider modernizing source code to ES6+ patterns (classes, const/let, async/await) to improve maintainability.

findings.title: ESLint in pipeline but no dedicated SAST or dependency scanning
findings.description: ESLint configured for static analysis and runs in CI pipeline. Package.json overrides patch known vulnerabilities. But no dedicated SAST tool or automated dependency scanning (Dependabot, Snyk, npm audit).
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST tool or automated dependency vulnerability scanning. ESLint provides code quality but not security-specific analysis.
findings.recommendation: Add .github/dependabot.yml for automated dependency alerts. Consider npm audit as a CI step.
